data.title: XRP Price Keeps Getting Shot Down as $1.45 Becomes 'No Man's Land'
data.description:
The XRP (XRP-USD) market is under the pump again. After yet another failed attempt to push above the $1.43–$1.45 resistance zone, the price has slipped to $1.41. This area, which has seen repeated rejections, is now acting like a "no man's land" for bulls, where every attempt to reclaim higher ground is met with immediate selling pressure. To make matters worse, trading volume during this recent slide spiked 74% above average, signaling that sellers are still in firm control of the short-term direction.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Sellers Maintain Control of Market Structure

The current price action is a continuation of a broader struggle that has defined XRP since its 2025 peak. While institutional interest, tracked through steady spot ETF inflows, has provided some support, it hasn't been enough to offset the persistent selling at these key psychological ceilings. This mismatch between long-term institutional buying and short-term speculative selling has trapped XRP in a tight, difficult-to-navigate corridor.

Traders Watch the Critical $1.40 Level

All eyes are now focused on the $1.40 support mark. This is the pivotal line of defense. If buyers can hold this floor, the market may stabilize, offering a chance to regroup for another attempt at breaking that $1.45 ceiling. However, if this level fails to hold, the path toward lower demand zones near $1.33, or even $1.00 in an extreme scenario, could quickly open up.

At the time of writing, XRP is sitting at $1.3956.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: SoftBank Races to Fund Massive OpenAI Stake amid Mounting Debt Pressure
data.description:
SoftBank (SFTBY) is looking to borrow up to $40 billion to keep pouring money into OpenAI. This would be the biggest dollar-based loan in the company's history, showing just how far founder Masayoshi Son is going to keep his firm at the center of the AI boom.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The loan is expected to last for one year. SoftBank is currently talking to banks like JPMorgan Chase (JPM) to get the deal done. This cash is meant to act as a bridge, giving the company the immediate funds it needs to meet its huge investment pledges while it manages its other assets.

Son Makes OpenAI the Main Focus of SoftBank

Masayoshi Son has made his bet on OpenAI the main focus of SoftBank. With this new $30 billion pledge, SoftBank's total investment in the AI maker is set to hit roughly $64.6 billion, giving it a 13% ownership stake. This means the Japanese firm's stock price is now tied directly to how well ChatGPT performs against rivals like Gemini and Anthropic.

To find the money for these massive payments, SoftBank has sold off its stake in Nvidia (NVDA), used its shares in Arm Holdings (ARM) and SoftBank Corp (JP:9434) to get loans, and spent billions on data centers and robotics to build out the physical tools needed for AI.

Analysts Express Growing Credit Concerns

The sheer amount of money being spent has made Wall Street nervous. This week, S&P Global Ratings lowered SoftBank's credit outlook from stable to negative. S&P analysts warned that OpenAI is now one of the riskier assets in SoftBank's portfolio, and the mounting debt needed to fund these bets is hurting the company's financial flexibility.

The cost of insuring against SoftBank defaulting on its debt has hit an 11-month high. While Son remains convinced that this is a once-in-a-lifetime chance to own the future of technology, investors are now waiting to see if a planned OpenAI initial public offering later this year can provide the cash boost needed to calm these fears.

Is SoftBank a Good Stock to Buy?

Since there are no analyst ratings for SoftBank, we utilized TipRanks' AI Analyst to rank the stock. The AI Analyst gave SFTBY a 'Neutral' rating of 61, noting that positive profitability and revenue are being offset by negative cash flows and leverage.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: U.S. AI Chip Curbs Could Hit Nvidia and AMD — But Which One Is More at Risk and Why?
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) slipped after reports that the ai-chip-exports">U.S. government is drafting new rules that could require companies to obtain approval before exporting AI chips abroad. The proposal would expand current restrictions and give Washington wider control over where advanced AI processors are sold.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Both companies make the chips that power large AI data centers. As firms race to build AI systems, demand for these processors has surged. But tighter export rules could slow some of that growth if shipments need government approval.

Nvidia: The Leader with the Most Exposure

Nvidia supplies many of the GPUs used to train and run modern AI models. Because of that, the company has the largest exposure if global export permits slow the rollout of new AI data centers.

Export limits have already affected Nvidia before. In Fiscal 2025, the company generated about $17 billion in revenue from China, roughly 13% of total sales, before U.S. restrictions limited shipments of its most advanced AI chips.

That dispute escalated when China responded by restricting the use of foreign processors in some government-backed data centers. Nearly a year later, Nvidia's advanced chip sales to China have still not fully resumed.

If new export permits are applied worldwide, large AI clusters outside the U.S. could face additional approvals before companies like Nvidia can ship their chips.

AMD Is Smaller but Still Expanding in AI

AMD is also growing its presence in the AI chip market with its data-center GPUs. The company reported $25.8 billion in revenue last year, as demand for AI computing continued to rise.

China is also an important market for AMD. In 2024, the company generated about $6.2 billion in revenue from China, which accounted for roughly 24% of its total sales. However, AMD's exposure to the AI accelerator market is still smaller than Nvidia's. That means it may face less immediate pressure if export approvals slow some large AI data-center builds.

At the same time, AMD is trying to win more business from cloud providers that want alternatives to Nvidia hardware.

What the New Rules Could Mean

The proposed system would create a tiered licensing process for AI chip exports.

  • Small clusters: Deployments of up to 1,000 GPUs would likely face a lighter review.
  • Large clusters: Bigger AI projects could require advance approval from U.S. officials before chips are shipped.
  • Massive deployments: Clusters of more than 200,000 GPUs may require involvement from the host government and strict security commitments to the U.S.

Officials said the rules are not meant to ban exports from companies like Nvidia or AMD. Instead, the goal is to control where the world's most powerful AI systems are built. The proposal is still in draft form, and the final rules could change.

Nvidia vs. AMD: Which AI Stock Is a Better Buy Now, According to Wall Street?

Using TipRanks' Stocks Comparison tool, we compared NVDA and AMD to see which AI chip stock analysts favor now. Nvidia currently holds a Strong Buy consensus rating, while AMD carries a Moderate Buy rating. Analysts also see notable upside for both stocks, with an average price target of $272.16 for Nvidia (about 48% upside) and $286.04 for AMD (around 43% upside).

Meanwhile, Nvidia also stands out with a TipRanks Smart Score of 10, compared with AMD's Smart Score of 6.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Corning Stock Slips as Broadcom CEO Downplays Need for Optical Tech in AI Data Centers
data.description:
Corning (GLW) investors felt the sting on Thursday as the fiber-optics leader saw its stock slide 6.97%. The drop followed comments from Broadcom (AVGO) CEO Hock Tan, who downplayed the immediate need for a shift to optical networking technology inside artificial intelligence data centers. This technology, specifically co-packaged optics (CPO), has been a major part of the bullish case for Corning, as it promises to connect AI chips using fiber instead of traditional copper.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Why Copper Is Still King for Now

During a conference call on Wednesday, Tan explained that while optical networking is great for connecting different server racks, it is not yet the right move for the scale-up process within the racks themselves. "Scaling up in a rack, in a cluster domain, you really want to use direct-attached copper as long as you can," Tan stated, noting that copper currently offers lower latency, less power consumption, and lower costs.

While this news hit Corning hard, it does not mean the fiber revolution is over. Industry experts and analysts generally agree that the shift to CPO is a question of "when," not "if." William Blair analyst Sebastien Naji noted that the market's reaction might be an overcorrection, as he views the move to optical networking as something that will likely take another two to three years to reach a "meaningful inflection" point.

Market Becomes Sensitive to Any News of a Delay

Investors have pushed Corning stock up 65% this year, betting that the company would emerge as a major winner in the AI build-out. Because of this high valuation, the market has become very sensitive to any news that suggests a delay in technology adoption.

It is worth noting that this is not a permanent setback for the company. Corning's leadership has continued to emphasize the long-term growth of fiber in data centers, pointing to recent wins like their major deal with Meta as proof that the demand remains strong. Even so, the reality remains that for now, the AI giants are still prioritizing the cost and efficiency of copper for their most immediate rack-level connections.

Is Corning Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to TipRanks, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Corning stock (GLW) based on nine Buy and three Hold recommendations. The average 12-month GLW stock price target of $136.30 indicates an upside potential of 1.2% from current levels.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: 'Microsoft Will Be the AI Winner,' Says Jefferies
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) remains well placed to capture rising enterprise spending on artificial intelligence, according to Jefferies analyst Brent Thill. He reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $675 price target. The analyst said Microsoft's platform across Azure and Microsoft 365 gives the company a strong edge as firms roll out AI tools.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Thill recently hosted Microsoft's head of investor relations and said the company's wide reach with large businesses could help it capture a bigger share of AI spending.

AI Could Expand the Microsoft 365 Market

The analyst said AI could increase the size of the Microsoft 365 market as firms add AI tools into daily work software. He also noted that AI margins are tracking ahead of cloud margins at a similar stage, which suggests the business could scale well as demand grows.

Thill said Microsoft's strategy does not depend on owning the best AI model. Instead, the company aims to control the platform where AI tools are built, run, and managed.

With more than 450 million paid Microsoft 365 users, Microsoft already controls many of the tools workers use every day. This gives the company a strong base to roll out AI features across its software.

The analyst also said Microsoft could benefit as AI agents begin to work across many apps. As those tools run tasks and call software systems through APIs, Microsoft may be able to earn more from its platform.

Valuation Still Looks Attractive

Thill added that Microsoft trades at about 21 times expected fiscal 2027 earnings, below its 10-year average of around 23.5 times. Given the company's stronger AI position today, the analyst believes the stock still has room to move higher.

Is Microsoft a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 33 Buys and three Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 3.47% rally in its share price over the past year, the average MSFT price target of $594.02 per share implies 44.64% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Why Are Stock Market Futures Rising Today, 3/5/26?
data.description:
U.S. stock futures inched higher Thursday night as oil prices eased after surging earlier in the day amid rising geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and disruptions near the Strait of Hormuz. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 Index (SPX) were up 0.11%, 0.16%, and 0.11%, respectively, at 8:03 p.m. EST on March 5.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Oil prices edged lower in late trading, with West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude down about 2.8% to around $78.7, while Brent crude traded near $85.4. Earlier in the session, however, oil prices had surged after traffic through the Strait of Hormuz came to a halt. WTI crude settled up 8.5% at $81.01, its highest level since 2024, while Brent crude jumped nearly 5%, putting crude on track for its biggest weekly gain since March 2022.

In the regular trading session on Thursday, the Dow, the S&P 500, and the Nasdaq fell 1.6%, 0.6%, and 0.3%, respectively. The declines erased part of Wednesday's strong rebound, when the Dow snapped a three‑day losing streak.

Looking ahead, traders await the release of the Nonfarm Payrolls report tomorrow. The market consensus expects a sharp deceleration to about 58,000 to 65,000 new jobs, down from 130,000 in the previous month.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: XRP Price Could Short Squeeze to $3.65 as Bearish Traders Overplay Their Hand
data.description:
The derivatives market is currently under a lot of pressure. Even though the XRP price (XRP-USD) is at $1.41, many traders on Binance have been placing heavy bets that the value will drop further.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

When too many people make the same bet, in this case, betting that the price will fall, it creates a risky situation for those traders. If the price moves even a little bit higher, they are forced to buy back their positions to stop their losses. This rush to buy pushes the price up even faster, which is known as a short squeeze.

Why Analysts Are Watching 2025 History

Analysts have noticed this exact setup before. In April 2025, when XRP was near $1.60, traders were just as bearish as they are today. That extreme pessimism was followed by a massive rally that pushed the price up to $3.65 by July. While nobody can guarantee this will happen again, it is a signal that experienced traders are watching closely.

The CLARITY Act Could Start the Rally

A crowded market provides the fuel for a potential jump, but it usually needs a reason to start. Right now, all eyes are on the CLARITY Act. This is a proposed law that aims to clear up confusion about how crypto is regulated in the U.S.

If this bill passes, it would finally give big institutional investors the green light to buy XRP. A major win for this bill combined with a market full of traders betting against XRP is exactly the kind of setup that fueled the big rally in 2025.

At the time of writing, XRP is sitting at $1.41.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: BitMine Stock Slips as Short Seller Culper Slams Ether and Tom Lee over "Death Spiral" Fears
data.description:
The debate over the future of Ethereum has turned hostile. In a report released Thursday, short-selling firm Culper Research announced it is betting against Ethereum (ETH-USD) and ETH-linked companies, specifically calling out BitMine Immersion Technologies (BMNR). BitMine's stock slipped 3.65% at market close.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Culper argues that Ethereum's network economics have fundamentally broken following the Fusaka upgrade in December 2025. According to the firm, this upgrade flooded the blockchain with excess space, which caused transaction fees to crater by roughly 90%. Because validators rely on these fees for income, Culper warns that the resulting drop in staking yields could force a negative feedback loop that weakens network security and drives users away.

Culper Challenges BitMine's Treasury Strategy

The report is aimed directly at BitMine, a company led by chairman Tom Lee that has turned into one of the largest corporate buyers of ether. BitMine has accumulated roughly 4.4 million ETH since July as part of an aggressive treasury strategy. However, the short seller points to data indicating that these holdings are currently 45% underwater, with BitMine sitting on approximately $7.4 billion in unrealized losses.

While Tom Lee has consistently defended the purchases by pointing to metrics like rising transaction counts and active addresses, Culper claims this data is misleading. Their analysis suggests that a large portion of that activity is actually the result of address poisoning scams, a tactic where attackers send tiny transactions to trick users into copying the wrong wallet addresses.

Culper Doubts Vitalik's Leadership

Culper also highlighted the recent moves of Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin, noting that he has sold nearly 20,000 ETH this year. The short seller used this to cast doubt on the confidence of Ethereum's leadership, stating, "Vitalik is selling, while bulls like Tom Lee are clueless as to ETH's new reality." The firm emphasized that its position is aligned with the co-founder's actions rather than the optimism often expressed by treasury firms. BitMine did not return a request for comment regarding the short report.

Is BitMine Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to TipRanks, BMNR stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on two Buy ratings from analysts assigned in the last three months. The average 12-month BMNR price target sits at $34.50, implying an upside potential of 69.5%.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Tumble as Oil Surges Above $80; Inflation Risks Return
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed in the red on Thursday as surging oil prices elevated inflation concerns.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • West Texas Intermediate (WTI) oil futures climbed to $81, registering the highest price since July 2024 as traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has dropped by over 95%.
  • Initial jobless claims for the week ended February 28 were 213,000, below the consensus estimate of 215,000 and unchanged from the prior week.
  • U.S. employers announced 48,307 job cuts in February, down by 72% year-over-year, according to outplacement firm Challenger, Gray & Christmas.
  • President Trump fired Department of Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem, who will be replaced by Oklahoma Republican Senator Markwayne Mullin on March 31.
  • The U.S. is drafting new rules that would ai-chip-exports">require government approval for global chip exports, sending Nvidia (NVDA) and AMD (AMD) lower.
  • Oracle (ORCL) is planning on laying off thousands of employees to offset AI infrastructure costs, according to Bloomberg.
  • The Trade Desk (TTD) was the top-performing S&P 500 (SPX) stock after its CEO, Jeff Green, disclosed a massive $148 million insider buy. The company is also reportedly in talks with OpenAI to sell ads on ChatGPT.

Today's Best-Performing Sector 

Energy was the best-performing sector on Thursday with a 0.59% gain as disruptions continued to ravage the Strait of Hormuz, which handles 20% of global oil flows. Alongside gains in WTI, crude oil futures (CL) climbed by 7%, while Brent crude futures (BZ) rose 3%. Furthermore, the U.S.-Iran war has also affected oil production and exports, with Saudi Arabia suspending operations at its largest oil refinery following an Iranian drone attack and China ordering domestic refiners to suspend gasoline and diesel exports.

Several energy stocks led the sector's gains, including:

  • Apa Corporation (APA)
  • EOG Resources (EOG)
  • Devon Energy (DVN)
  • Chevron (CVX)
  • Coterra Energy (CTRA)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, materials was the worst-performing sector due to inflation risks from higher energy prices. Inflation fears have also lowered rate cut odds, which hurts commodity producers and materials companies because they are viewed as economically sensitive and cyclical. In addition, both gold (XAUUSD) and silver (XAGUSD) posted losses, pressuring miners.

Notable materials stocks trading lower include:

  • Martin Marietta Materials (MLM)
  • Sherwin-Williams Company (SHW)
  • Ecolab (ECL)
  • Vulcan Materials (VMC)
  • Linde (LIN)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Nvidia and AMD Stock Dip as U.S. Drafts Global AI Chip Export Permits
data.description:
The U.S. government is preparing to take a massive role in the global tech industry by acting as the primary gatekeeper for AI power. According to a Bloomberg exclusive, federal officials have written draft regulations that would restrict shipments of AI accelerators to any country without prior approval from Washington. This move would give the U.S. broad control over which nations are allowed to build the data centers needed to train and run advanced artificial intelligence.

  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Investor nerves were already visible on Thursday morning, as indicated in the image below, with Nvidia stock (NVDA) down 0.67% in pre-market trading, while AMD stock (AMD) closed down 1.3% following the news.
